definition: a machine designed to carry out tasks automatically
hook: The word robot is barely a hundred years old.
modernAnchor: You ask one to vacuum your floor. You picture one driving the car.
journey: The word was coined for a 1920 play by Czech writer Karel Čapek titled R.U.R.
Čapek needed a word for artificial workers. His brother Josef suggested robota: the Czech word for unpaid drudgery serfs performed for their nobles.
Before R.U.R., no one called automated machines robots.
The play was translated into thirty languages within five years. Science fiction never recovered.
